situation: audit-2026-07-04-002 finding MM-H004 (priority 4, route-fix): api.py L1371-1397 run_agent_action expands untrusted payload.payload via **args into 12 engine methods. Attacker can inject arbitrary keyword arguments (e.g. import_baostock_snapshot(path='/etc/passwd') or create_rule with unexpected params) because no per-action parameter validation exists at the API layer.
action: Added _ACTION_KWARGS_WHITELIST dict mapping each action name to its allowed parameter set (derived from engine method signatures). Added _filter_action_args helper that rejects unexpected keys with HTTP 400 listing the offending parameters. Applied filter before all **kwargs expansion points. update_rule/update_strategy_session whitelists include the popped rule_id/session_id keys so filtering doesn't strip them.
outcome_evidence: Commit a8ee972 pushed to origin/auto/opt-moni-master-1783105913. Whitelist covers all 12 **kwargs actions: import_qlib_snapshot(3 keys), create_rule(6), update_rule(7 incl rule_id), create_strategy_session(6), update_strategy_session(7 incl session_id), run_strategy_once(4), run_backtest_qlib(5), run_backtest_batch(15), create_replay_session(6), get_qlib_history(3), settle_day(2). Non-**kwargs actions (place_order/cancel_order/set_data_source/update_settings/delete_rule/delete_strategy_session/evaluate_rules/advance_trade_date) use explicit key access and are not vulnerable.
next_suggestion: 派生 fix MM-M009 (priority 12, /llm/chat SSRF + credential theft) — 同文件 api.py，可在同一 worktree 追加修复

audit_findings.finding: /agent/action **kwargs expansion from untrusted input enables parameter injection into 12 engine methods
audit_findings.remediation: Per-action parameter whitelist with reject-on-unknown-keys enforcement applied before **kwargs expansion
